knowledge_atoms.title: Railway PORT must never be hardcoded
knowledge_atoms.core: Setting PORT as a Railway environment variable overrides Railway's dynamic port injection and kills external routing. Always delete PORT from Railway variables and let Railway inject it automatically.
knowledge_atoms.proof: DevPilot backend showed 'Application failed to respond' until PORT=3001 variable was deleted from Railway

knowledge_atoms.title: connect-pg-simple createTableIfMissing prevents boot crash
knowledge_atoms.core: Session store initialization crashes the entire Express server if the sessions table doesn't exist. Use createTableIfMissing:true to auto-create it on boot.
knowledge_atoms.proof: DevPilot backend crashed repeatedly until createTableIfMissing was added

knowledge_atoms.title: DATABASE_URL public proxy URL for Termux external connections
knowledge_atoms.core: Railway internal postgres.railway.internal URL only works inside Railway network. From Termux, always use the public proxy URL containing proxy.rlwy.net for database connections.
knowledge_atoms.proof: Migration failed with ENOTFOUND postgres.railway.internal until public URL was used
knowledge_atoms.code_application: all Railway PostgreSQL migrations from Termux

knowledge_atoms.title: ALLOWED_ORIGINS must match exact Vercel subdomain
knowledge_atoms.core: Railway CORS ALLOWED_ORIGINS must include the full Vercel URL including any subdomain prefix. A mismatch silently blocks all API calls from the frontend.
knowledge_atoms.proof: DevPilot login failed until ALLOWED_ORIGINS was updated to match exact Vercel deployment URL

knowledge_atoms.title: Git PAT must be embedded in remote URL for Termux push
knowledge_atoms.core: GitHub authentication from Termux requires the PAT embedded directly in the remote URL: https://username:TOKEN@github.com/org/repo.git. The credential helper alone is insufficient.
knowledge_atoms.proof: All git push attempts failed until PAT was embedded in remote URL

knowledge_atoms.title: Vercel Next.js vulnerability blocks require version upgrade
knowledge_atoms.core: Vercel hard-blocks deployment of Next.js versions with known CVEs. Upgrade to latest patched version and delete PORT variable — these are the two most common Vercel deploy blockers.
knowledge_atoms.proof: DevPilot frontend blocked on Next.js 15.0.3 CVE until upgraded to 14.2.29

knowledge_atoms.title: DevPilot three-tier SaaS architecture pattern
knowledge_atoms.core: Free tier (3 repos, core deploy), Pro tier ($9/mo, unlimited + AI audit/fix/chat), Team tier ($29/mo, multi-user shared vault). License keys DPLT-XXXX format gate tier access server-side.
knowledge_atoms.proof: Built and deployed DevPilot v1.0 with this exact tier system

knowledge_atoms.title: Railway DB migration from Termux using public URL
knowledge_atoms.core: Run migrations from Termux with DATABASE_URL set to the public proxy URL: DATABASE_URL='postgresql://...' node backend/db/migrate.js from inside the backend folder where pg is installed.
knowledge_atoms.proof: Migration succeeded after switching to public URL and running from backend/ directory

knowledge_atoms.title: FanzAgent v5.2 AI Brain system architecture
knowledge_atoms.core: Brain persists in brain.json as portable knowledge store. Cosine similarity blocks duplicate atoms. Universal extraction prompt works on any AI. brain ingest/ask/search/stats/push commands form the complete knowledge loop.
knowledge_atoms.proof: Built and deployed FanzAgent v5.2 with full brain system, ingested 50 atoms

knowledge_atoms.title: DevPilot full stack build order
knowledge_atoms.core: Build order: 1) Backend routes+services, 2) DB migration, 3) Socket.IO terminal streaming, 4) Frontend pages, 5) Deploy backend to Railway, 6) Deploy frontend to Vercel, 7) Wire ALLOWED_ORIGINS, 8) Run migration via public URL
knowledge_atoms.proof: Successfully deployed DevPilot v1.0 following this exact order
knowledge_atoms.code_application: DevPilot and all future fullstack projects

build_decisions.decision: Delete PORT variable from Railway — never hardcode it
build_decisions.reasoning: Railway injects PORT dynamically. Hardcoding overrides it and blocks external traffic.
Prevents boot crash when sessions table doesn't exist yet

build_decisions.decision: DevPilot tier system: Free/Pro/Team with DPLT- license keys
build_decisions.reasoning: Simplest monetization that gates AI features behind Pro without requiring Stripe on day one

sessions.summary: Batch ingested 50 knowledge documents from FanzOS Master Brain v5 package covering Avatar, AI Systems, Anthropic, Computation, Mobile/Termux, Ethics and Safety.

Built and deployed DevPilot v1.0 — full SaaS product from Android phone. Discovered 10 critical patterns for Railway+Vercel deployment. Installed FanzAgent v5.2 with AI Brain system. Ingested 50 FanzOS knowledge atoms. THE ONE Brain lifecycle system planned as C: private master instance + Pro user instances.

compound_theories.theory: The PORT+CORS+Migration triple — when a Railway+Vercel app fails silently, check these three in order: (1) PORT not hardcoded, (2) ALLOWED_ORIGINS matches exact Vercel URL, (3) DATABASE_URL uses public proxy for external connections. These three variables account for 90% of Railway+Vercel connection failures.
